title: Appellate Quarterly - April 2025
content:
## Appellate Quarterly - April 2025
Join McCarthy Tétrault’s National Appellate Litigation Group as we discuss appeals of importance to the Canadian business community during the last quarter. Each session brings you up-to-date on recent appellate developments across Canada. Topics to be discussed this quarter include:
- Supreme Court of Canada: When does a multiplicity of proceedings amount to an abuse of process?
- Federal: Federal Court of Appeal clarifies whether and when to bring a judicial review when there is a statutory right of appeal.
- Atlantic: Which jurisdiction’s law governs non-pecuniary damages in a cross-border tort?
- Québec: Quebec Court of Appeal reiterates the duty to negotiate in good faith when applying a renewal clause but expresses skepticism about whether it can lead to an order for specific performance rather than damages.
- West: What is the role of negotiation evidence in interpreting contracts?
Speakers include: Connor Bildfell , Laura Gill , Kevan Hanowski , Jean-Philippe Mathieu and Byron Shaw
Note: All instructions and information on how to access the webinar will be forwarded a few days before the event. This program qualifies for up to 1.5 hours of credit under the mandatory education regimes in British Columbia and Ontario. Barreau du Québec Continuing Education: This program contains up to 1.5 hours of content. For more information regarding the declaration and recognition of educational activities, please contact the Barreau du Québec . For questions about this seminar, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca .
